verifiedNumbers.belgiumPick: Belgium ML +180, 1.5u, SHARP/WEAK, proven wallets 8 FOR / 6 AGAINST, 8:00 PM ET

verifiedNumbers.sinceJune1: 211-167, +54.2u (do NOT lead with this again — used in weekend recap draft)

hero.screenshot: Belgium ML pick card (tier, units, sharp consensus visible)
hero.postWindow: 12:00-13:00 ET — the USA-Belgium conversation builds all day; catch lunch scroll, babysit replies 30 min
hero.text:
The US plays a World Cup knockout game at home tonight, and the bettors we track are leaning Belgium.

I'm American. I wanted to skip this one. But we didn't build a tracker to follow it only when it's comfortable — the wallets we follow are 21-8 on this World Cup, and more of them are on Belgium at +180 than on the US.

It's a small position, not a max play. The system's top-rated bet tonight is actually Arizona +101 on the baseball side.

Money moves all day, so the live board is ahead of this tweet — picks lock 15 minutes before kickoff.

Honest question: could you bet against your own country in a knockout game?
hero.rtLine: We didn't build a tracker to follow it only when it's comfortable.
hero.selfReplyAt25min: For transparency, it's genuinely split — 8 of the winning wallets we track are on Belgium, 6 stayed with the US. That's exactly why it's sized small. The live board has the current read: nhlsavant.com/?ref=t0706b

gradePosts.win:
Belgium +180 cashed and nobody in this house was cheering. Strangest win of the tournament.

Soccer card: 22-8, up 26 units since group play. We bet the number, not the logo — even when the logo is ours.
gradePosts.loss:
The US moves on, and our Belgium ticket dies with the party. Genuinely fine eating this one.

Soccer card: 21-9, still up 22 units on the tournament. Some losses don't feel like losses.

alternates.text:
Quiet Monday board, one thing worth saying out loud: the system's top tier tonight is Arizona at +101.

Plus money, and the only proven winners with a position on the game are both on the Diamondbacks. Nobody we track is on the other side.

First pitch 9:41 ET. Money moves until then — the board locks 15 minutes out.

Small slates are where the discipline shows.
alternates.rtLine: Small slates are where the discipline shows.
alternates.note: Use if you'd rather not touch the USA fade. Honest per board: proven 2-0 ARI.

alternates.text: (the weekend recap hero from ready_to_post/2026-07-06_0915_weekend_recap.json — still strong if you haven't posted it; post it FIRST and push the Belgium hero to ~5 PM)

replyTargets.theirAngle: USA vs Belgium picks/odds preview (large media account)
replyTargets.reply: Interesting wrinkle from the money side: the winning bettors we track are split on this one, and slightly more of them are on Belgium at +180 than on the US. First time this tournament they've leaned against the home crowd.

replyTargets.theirAngle: USA-Belgium RO16 preview, Balogun team news
replyTargets.reply: The Balogun news moved the price, but the wallets we track still lean Belgium. Saying that as an American who hates typing it.
The winning money we track was all over Spain in the group stage — tonight it didn't reach consensus on this game at all. Sometimes the quiet is the read.
The quiet one on tonight's board: Arizona +101. The only proven winners we track with a position on that game are both on the Diamondbacks.

doNotDo: Do not lead with +54u / 211-167 again (weekend recap already used it)
Do not overstate the Belgium lean — it is 8-6 split, small position, and the hero must say so
Do not quote Belgium/Arizona numbers after ~7 PM without re-running socialBoard.mjs
No link or record-dropping in the replies — curiosity does the work
